message:
chore(beads): file mcp/connector tier-2 unblock prerequisites (ft-y0loj.5)

Per docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md,
the parked mcp/* and connector_* tier-2 extractions stay cycle-blocked
until the four leaf-types extractions land. The proposal lists items
1–3 (error/config/policy) as already shipped — items 4 (cass + caut)
and the connector in-core importer audit had not been filed.

Three new beads:

  - ft-8cg6y  [P2] extract frankenterm-core-cass-types leaf crate
  - ft-2z15d  [P2] extract frankenterm-core-caut-types leaf crate
  - ft-dfd16  [P3] resolve in-core connector_* importers
              (policy.rs / config.rs / chaos_scale_harness.rs /
               runtime_telemetry.rs hold connector_*_TelemetrySnapshot
               fields directly — substantive consumption that requires
               either a leaf-types crate or per-field rewriting before
               the cluster can move out)

Each ft-y0loj.5.D.* bead carries a concrete extraction recipe:
exact line ranges to move, what to leave behind (Client impls + IO
glue + cfg(test) blocks), in-core re-export shape that keeps every
existing crate::cass::* / crate::caut::* call site resolving via the
parent module, target Cargo.toml dep set (mirrors the proven ft-usvnt
+ ft-g6sa8 pattern), and reverse-dep audit of the consumers that
will pass through unchanged.

Dep edges: ft-8cg6y → ft-dfd16 and ft-2z15d → ft-dfd16 (the
connector importer audit waits for the leaf-types pattern to be
proven on cass + caut first; same sequencing the proposal mandates).

No source changes — these beads are the audit deliverable for this
session per the dispatch's option (b).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2xbhx","title":"[refactor][LOW] mcp_helpers::build_policy_engine is dead code after ft-eu0no refactor","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of f540a5d5 (ft-eu0no P1 rate limiter share).

Defect:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_helpers.rs:97 mcp_helpers::build_policy_engine() now has zero callers after the shared-limiter variant in mcp.rs:348 superseded it
- Risk: attractive nuisance — a future tool author grabs this one via autocomplete and reintroduces the fresh-per-request rate-limiter bug that ft-eu0no fixed

Fix: delete mcp_helpers::build_policy_engine entirely. Verify no external crate is calling it via grep/cargo-semver.

Reviewer: post-session orchestrator fresh-eyes pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:41:58.572366Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:35:47.088905Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T02:35:47.088610Z","close_reason":"False positive — reviewer agent hallucinated path. build_policy_engine actually lives at crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:324 (NOT mcp_helpers.rs:97 as the bead claimed) and IS called at mcp.rs:353 by build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter. Not dead code — it is the base helper that the shared-limiter variant delegates to. No action needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-2z15d","title":"[ft-y0loj.5.D.2] extract frankenterm-core-caut-types leaf crate (mcp/connector unblock prerequisite)","description":"Companion to ft-8cg6y (cass-types). Per docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md prerequisite item 4, extract the caut type definitions into a leaf crate below frankenterm-core to unblock mcp/connector tier-2 extraction.\n\nScope (move from crates/frankenterm-core/src/caut.rs into new crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/src/lib.rs):\n  - CautService enum + impl + helper fns is_openai_slug/is_anthropic_slug/is_google_slug + Display impl (lines 23-122)\n  - CautUsage struct (line 124)\n  - CautRefresh struct (line 137)\n  - CautAccountUsage struct (line 150)\n  - CautError enum + impl (lines 173-229)\n\nLEAVE in caut.rs:\n  - CautClient struct + impl (line 231+) — uses Command, Redactor, std::sync::Mutex, std::process IO\n  - parse_json / parse_usage_json / parse_refresh_json / categorize_io_error helpers (line 405+) — they consume Redactor\n  - The whole CautV1 envelope conversion pipeline (line 487+) — IO-bound, stays with the client\n\nCargo.toml deps: serde, serde_json, std (mirror frankenterm-core-error-types).\n\nIn-core re-exports: parent caut.rs adds re-export of types-crate so existing crate::caut::CautError, crate::caut::CautService, etc. keep resolving. New crate is a one-way path-dep of frankenterm-core.\n\nReverse-dep audit (consumers that will keep working through the re-export):\n  - mcp_error.rs (line 4, 156): crate::caut::CautError\n  - mcp_helpers.rs (line 11): crate::caut::CautService\n  - mcp.rs (line 38): crate::caut::CautError\n  - mcp_tools.rs (line 4422): crate::caut::CautService::supported_cli_inputs()\n  - workflows/mod.rs (lines 7378, 7832-7865): CautError/CautRefresh/CautAccountUsage/CautService — all type-only consumption, will resolve via re-export\n  - workflows/account_steps.rs (lines 32, 68, 109, 110, 124): CautError/CautClient/CautService/CautRefresh/CautAccountUsage — note CautClient stays in core but the rest move\n  - workflows/builtin_workflows.rs (line 844): CautClient::new() — stays in core\n  - wezterm.rs (line 2167): doc comment only\n\nSuccess criteria:\n  1. workspace builds clean via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-swarm-<slug>-target).\n  2. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib caut:: passes (kept tests).\n  3. New crate cargo check standalone.\n  4. Unblocks ft-y0loj.5 mcp tier-2 extraction alongside ft-8cg6y.\n\nReference: docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md §'Prerequisite work' item 4.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:27:41.147924Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:47.161217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","frankenterm-core","modularity","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2z15d","depends_on_id":"ft-dfd16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:47.160648Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-2zbie","title":"[review][MEDIUM] 9 placeholder goldens from d399a957 never regenerated — mission_tx conformance harness not exercising its own schema fence","description":"Systemic finding audit round 17. d399a957 shipped mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs with 9 placeholder goldens (3-byte {} each) for wa.mission_*/wa.tx_* tools. Commit documented first test run would panic-with-regenerate-hint or populate via UPDATE_GOLDEN=1. 9 hours later all 9 files still 3 bytes. No regenerate commit. Two explanations: tests never ran (correlates with 3 'verification partial' commits cec75f71/4e270e95/8d1e93dc and ft-4o5mb.1 shipping broken per ft-r3mmz HIGH) or tests ran and failed without notice. mcp is in default features so cargo test SHOULD touch this. Signal lost: any wa.mission_*/wa.tx_* response shape can drift freely. Fix: agent with working rch lane runs UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mcp_conformance_mission_tx --features mcp,asupersync-runtime. Related not-filed: FakeWezterm duplicated across 3 test files; SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH had 2 back-to-back bugs. MEDIUM test-surface. Audit 17 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:25.307818Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:46:30.524467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:46:30.524300Z","close_reason":"Resolved: 9 mission/tx goldens are regenerated and no longer placeholders","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-31kaq","title":"proptest(ingest): pin Gap content == current and Content delta.len() < current.len() invariants","description":"proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs pins MR1-MR5 + MR3b (overlap existence) on extract_delta. Two additional shipping contracts are implicit but unchecked: (MR7) every Gap variant returned must carry content == current (the three return sites at ingest.rs:1675, 1713, 1724 all satisfy this, but nothing prevents a future refactor from breaking it); (MR8) when Content(delta) is returned, delta.len() < current.len() strictly — since overlap_len >= 1 for any match in the memchr path. Add both as proptest properties alongside the existing MRs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:20.655704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:02:49.872719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:02:49.872352Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6ff97e37 (test(proptest/ingest): add MR7 Gap-content-equals-current and MR8 Content-strictly-shorter invariants). Concurrent-agent commit landed the exact same MR7/MR8 additions I wrote; my da87c368 ended up as a beads-tracker-only commit (four pre-existing blockers: ft-uigpj, ft-s2u51, ft-zv3u9 filed).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-84ulk","title":"workflow runner releases pane lock on pre-start cx cancellation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:07:27.230187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:17:04.275236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:17:04.274845Z","close_reason":"Released pane lock and marked execution failed when run_workflow_with_cx sees a pre-start cancelled child context; added regression coverage for the persisted-record + lock cleanup path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-87tdq","title":"[testing][metamorphic] patterns.rs PatternEngine metamorphic relations (5 MRs + 1 shipped proptest)","description":"## Context

Applies /testing-metamorphic methodology to `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` (`PatternEngine::detect`).

An existing metamorphic suite `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs` covers the fast-path byte scanner `pattern_trigger::TriggerScanner` (idempotence, empty-input neutrality, prefix-shift invariance, concat subadditivity, case-insensitive equality, locate bounds). The higher-level rule-based `PatternEngine` at patterns.rs:2196 is NOT covered.

## Metamorphic relations for PatternEngine::detect

**MR1 — Idempotence (SHIPPED in this bead)**: calling `engine.detect(x)` twice on the same engine must return a byte-identical detection set. A regression in engine state (e.g. a counter/LRU that taints later calls, a dirty telemetry mutation that reshapes output) fails this.

**MR2 — Construction determinism**: two engines built via `PatternEngine::new()` run against the same input must produce identical detection sets. Catches any builtin-pack load order non-determinism or hash-map iteration leakage.

**MR3 — Empty input neutrality**: `engine.detect("")` returns an empty vec. Trivial but cheap, and catches anchor-free rules that spuriously match zero-length input.

**MR4 — Benign-prefix/suffix monotonicity**: appending (or prepending) a string known to produce zero detections must never ADD detections to the result. Formally: if `detect(b) == []` then `detect(x) ⊆ detect(x + b)`. Catches boundary-drift bugs where a benign extension unexpectedly triggers a rule that wasn't matching the original.

**MR5 — Rule reordering (set-equivalence)**: permuting rules within a pack (via `with_packs` on a hand-built test pack) must produce the same detection set when normalized by (rule_id, span). Any order-dependence is a real bug in aho-corasick composition or dedup logic — aligns with the memory note about "Aho-Corasick LeftmostFirst non-overlapping matching is not composable".

**MR6 — Unicode wrap invariance**: wrapping input with non-ASCII unicode that contains no rule anchor bytes (e.g. "\u{1F600}" emoji at either end) must not change detections. The anchors are ASCII in the shipped pack, so unicode that doesn't include anchor bytes is transparent.

## Why MR1 for the shipped proptest

Cheapest, highest-signal. Catches any non-determinism or accidental mutable state in the engine without false positives: any correct stateless pattern-matcher must satisfy it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs` ships with MR1 as a proptest
- Strategy generates a realistic mix of printable ASCII + newlines, 0-512 bytes
- Detection set compared via a stable canonical key (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) regardless of internal vec ordering
- Bead left OPEN for a follow-up agent to implement MR2-MR6 or a subset

Ship discipline: create a new test file (no collision with the existing `metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs`, which covers a different engine).

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /testing-metamorphic application, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:16:45.718918Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167479Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167311Z","close_reason":"Shipped in f11ca522: MR1 (idempotence) as two proptest cases in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs — same-engine repeat-call equality + fresh-engine agreement on the same input. 80/20 printable-ASCII+newline strategy, canonical (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) projection. MR2-MR6 (construction determinism, empty-input neutrality, benign-suffix monotonicity, rule reordering, unicode wrap invariance) documented in bead body; follow-up panes can implement them without re-opening the bead since they're incremental additions to the same file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metamorphic","patterns","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-89fl5","title":"[bug] BackpressureManager::evaluate tier + tier_entered_at writes are non-atomic; concurrent reader can bypass hysteresis","description":"# Bug

backpressure.rs:546-550 applies a tier transition via two separate
lock acquisitions:

    *self.current_tier.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = proposed;
    *self
        .tier_entered_at
        .write()
        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Instant::now();

Between these two writes, a concurrent evaluate() on a second
thread can observe `current_tier = proposed` but `tier_entered_at =
<old-timestamp>`. That thread's hysteresis check at line 540:

    let entered = *self.tier_entered_at.read()...;
    let elapsed_ms = u64::try_from(entered.elapsed().as_millis())...;
    if elapsed_ms < self.config.hysteresis_ms {
        return None;
    }

uses the STALE `entered` value. Elapsed time is computed against the
PREVIOUS tier's entry time, which is far past the hysteresis window —
so the downgrade is allowed when it shouldn't be.

# Scenario

1. T=0: tier=Green, entered_at=T0.
2. T=100s: T1 evaluate() proposes Red (upgrade). Writes
   current_tier=Red. Scheduler pauses T1 before `tier_entered_at`
   write.
3. T=100.001s: T2 evaluate() reads current_tier=Red, proposes Green
   (downgrade). Hysteresis check reads entered_at = T0, elapsed =
   100s ≫ hysteresis_ms → downgrade allowed. Writes
   current_tier=Green.
4. T=100.002s: T1 resumes, writes tier_entered_at=100s.

Final state: current_tier=Green, tier_entered_at=100s. Red tier was
entered AND downgraded without the hysteresis window ever running.

# When does this matter

BackpressureManager takes `&self` everywhere so it's shareable
across threads (`Arc<BackpressureManager>`). Today there's only one
active evaluator task in the runtime (runtime.rs:3803), but any
future concurrent caller — stats reader that preemptively classifies,
a backpressure-aware admission gate on the dispatch path, a second
worker loop for bursty load — would race.

Worth fixing now because (a) the API promises `&self` multi-thread
safety, and (b) the fix is ~5 lines.

# Fix

Collapse `current_tier: RwLock<BackpressureTier>` and
`tier_entered_at: RwLock<Instant>` into a single
`RwLock<(BackpressureTier, Instant)>` so reads + writes see the tuple
as a unit. All existing accessors (`current_tier()`,
`evaluate()`) take one lock instead of two.

# Test

`concurrent_evaluate_never_produces_stale_entered_at` — spawn 2
threads repeatedly evaluating against known-bouncing depths; assert
that AFTER each observed transition, the tier and entered_at are
consistent (entered_at is at or after the transition moment).

A full race-demonstration test is loom-level infrastructure;
an observable post-condition invariant is shippable today.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:29.956478Z","created_by":"pane3-bp-atomic","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","concurrency","hysteresis"]}
+{"id":"ft-8cg6y","title":"[ft-y0loj.5.D.1] extract frankenterm-core-cass-types leaf crate (mcp/connector unblock prerequisite)","description":"Per docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md, mcp/* and connector_* tier-2 extractions are cycle-blocked: those clusters have heavy forward deps on core types (Config/Error/Policy/CassError/CautError/etc.) AND back-edges into core. Cargo rejects the path-dep loop. Fix template proven by ft-usvnt (resource-types) + ft-g6sa8 / ft-otfxs / ft-0pykm (error/config/policy types extractions): move JUST the type definitions into leaf crates *below* core so cluster + core both reach down to the leaf without crossing each other.\n\nThis bead extracts the cass-types leaf.\n\nScope (move from crates/frankenterm-core/src/cass.rs into new crates/frankenterm-core-cass-types/src/lib.rs):\n  - CassAgent enum (line 27) + impl + Display impl (lines 36-93)\n  - CassSearchResult struct (line 96)\n  - CassSearchHit struct (line 123)\n  - CassSession struct (line 144)\n  - CassMessage struct (line 163)\n  - CassSessionSummary struct + impl (lines 178-258)\n  - CassViewResult struct (line 261)\n  - CassContextLine struct (line 282)\n  - CassIndexResult struct (line 295)\n  - CassStatus struct (line 314)\n  - CassError enum + impl (lines 333-403)\n  - SearchOptions / ViewOptions structs (lines 405-429)\n  - CassExportQuery + Default + helpers (lines 431-469)\n  - CassContentExportQuery + Default + helpers (lines 471-499)\n  - CassExportSessionRecord struct (line 502)\n  - CassExportContentChunk struct (line 532)\n\nLEAVE in cass.rs:\n  - CassClient struct + impl (line 751+) — consumes StorageHandle, Redactor, Command, AgentProvider — heavy in-crate deps\n  - export_sessions async fn (line 553) and other recorder-export glue — depends on StorageHandle\n  - the whole #[cfg(test)] block (line 1436+)\n\nCargo.toml: only serde, serde_json, std deps (mirror frankenterm-core-error-types). Wire into workspace [members].\n\nIn-core re-exports: parent cass.rs adds  so existing  etc. keep resolving. New crate is a path-dep of frankenterm-core (one-way). The 4 in-core consumers (mcp_error.rs, mcp_helpers.rs, mcp.rs, mcp_tools.rs) keep using  paths — the re-export hides the move.\n\nSuccess criteria:\n  1. workspace builds clean: cargo check --workspace --all-targets via rch.\n  2. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cass:: passes (kept tests).\n  3. New crate cargo check passes standalone.\n  4. Unblocks ft-y0loj.5 (mcp tier-2 extraction) for re-attempt.\n\nReference: docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md §'Prerequisite work' item 4.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:27:19.529314Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:45.974364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","frankenterm-core","modularity","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-8cg6y","depends_on_id":"ft-dfd16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:45.973752Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-8ihuz","title":"session_restore::load_scrollback_data loads ALL segments up to max_seq before truncating, memory-blows on large scrollbacks","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:705-751 load_scrollback_data queries 'SELECT content FROM output_segments WHERE pane_id = ?1 AND seq <= ?2 ORDER BY seq ASC' with NO LIMIT. For a pane with a large scrollback checkpoint (hundreds of MB to multiple GB is realistic for agents running for days), every restore reads the full content into memory as a Vec<String>, THEN calls scrollback.truncate(max_lines) at line 746 which drops everything except the last N. Default restore_max_lines = 5000 (session_restore.rs:142), so for 1M segments totaling 1 GB, we allocate 1 GB and keep ~5 KB. The allocation spike can OOM frankenterm on startup of a large session. Fix: reverse the ORDER BY and push the LIMIT into SQL so the query returns at most max_lines rows from the tail, then reverse in Rust to restore chronological order. This bounds memory at max_lines × avg_segment_size (~ tens of MB worst case). Same truncation semantics (keep most-recent lines) preserved.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:04.442611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:49.985139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:49.984775Z","close_reason":"Landed in 459fadc4 (fix(session_restore): bound load_scrollback_data by max_lines via SQL LIMIT). Flipped ORDER BY to DESC, added LIMIT ?3 with max_lines, reversed in Rust to restore chronological order. Regression test load_scrollback_data_respects_max_lines_limit_ft_8ihuz pins 5-of-50 clamp + chronological order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8jbpj","title":"wezterm.rs pre-existing compile breakage blocks ft-e34d9.10.5.2","description":"While validating ft-e34d9.10.5.2 on 2026-04-21, the checkout surfaced pre-existing source breakage already present in HEAD.

That leaves the control-flow expression malformed and blocks frankenterm-core compile/hygiene in the vendored mux verification lane.

Evidence:

 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.4","title":"E2E proof for ft tx run: execute a real transactional multi-pane operation and verify audit trail","description":"# Goal
Prove that ft tx run executes real pane operations transactionally, including compensation on failure, with a complete audit trail.

# Background
The tx integration tests (ft-y9lnb.5) use MockWezterm. This E2E test uses real (or headless mux server) panes to prove the full CLI->executor->pane->audit pipeline works.

# Verified Contract Format (MissionTxContract JSON)
{
  "tx_version": 1,
  "intent": { "tx_id": "test-tx-1", "requested_by": "operator", "summary": "E2E test", "correlation_id": "e2e-001", "created_at_ms": <timestamp> },
  "plan": {
    "plan_id": "plan-1", "tx_id": "test-tx-1",
    "steps": [
      { "step_id": "step-1", "ordinal": 0, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo step1-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 0" },
      { "step_id": "step-2", "ordinal": 1, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 1, "text": "echo step2-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 1" }
    ],
    "preconditions": [],
    "compensations": [
      { "for_step_id": "step-1", "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo COMPENSATED" } }
    ]
  },
  "lifecycle_state": "pending",
  "outcome": "pending",
  "receipts": []
}

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_tx_run.py

### Scenario 1: Happy path (2-step transaction)
1. Start ft watch with headless mux server
2. Create 2 panes with shell sessions
3. Write mission.json contract (template above)
4. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
5. Verify: ft robot get-text 0 contains 'step1-marker'
6. Verify: ft robot get-text 1 contains 'step2-marker'
7. Verify: ft tx show --include-contract shows committed state

### Scenario 2: Partial failure with compensation
1. Same setup, but step 2 targets nonexistent pane 99
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission_fail.json
3. Verify: pane 0 contains 'COMPENSATED' (compensation ran)
4. Verify: ft tx show shows compensation state
5. Verify: ft audit shows failure + compensation records

### Scenario 3: Policy denial
1. Configure ft.toml safety to deny SendText on pane 1
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
3. Verify: prepare-phase denial with policy reason in output
4. Verify: pane 0 does NOT contain 'step1-marker' (no steps executed on denial)

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_tx_run_<timestamp>.json
- Per-scenario: scenario_name, contract_file, ft_tx_run_stdout, ft_tx_run_stderr, ft_tx_show_output, audit_records, pane_contents, pass/fail
- On failure: full diagnostic dump

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 3 scenarios pass.
2. Happy path shows markers in panes AND committed state in ft tx show.
3. Compensation scenario shows compensation execution in audit trail AND pane output.
4. Policy denial shows zero pane modifications.
5. Completes in under 60 seconds.
6. Structured JSON report produced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108953Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","proof","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.740774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.533076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"ft-dfd16","title":"[ft-y0loj.5.connector] resolve in-core connector_* importers before connector tier-2 extraction","description":"Per docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md, connector_* tier-2 extraction is blocked by FOUR in-core files that substantively consume connector types — separate from the cycle issue that the leaf-types extractions (ft-8cg6y/ft-2z15d) solve.\n\nThe importers (count from proposal Appendix):\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs            — 8 connector_* type refs\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/config.rs            — 10 connector_* type refs\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/chaos_scale_harness.rs — 2 connector_* refs\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_telemetry.rs — 6 connector_* refs\n\nproposal example (policy.rs):\n  pub credential_broker: crate::connector_credential_broker::CredentialBrokerTelemetrySnapshot\n  pub connector_governor: crate::connector_governor::GovernorSnapshot\n  pub connector_registry: crate::connector_registry::RegistryTelemetrySnapshot\n\nThis is substantive consumption (struct fields), not a façade. After the cluster moves out, these in-core files would either need to import from the new crate (creating the cargo cycle) or have their connector-specific fields broken out.\n\nRecommended fix pattern:\n  1. Audit the connector telemetry snapshot types each in-core file consumes.\n  2. Decide per-type: (a) is it a pure data type that should ride a leaf-types crate (frankenterm-core-connector-types) below core, or (b) does it logically belong to the cluster and the core consumer should be rewritten to NOT hold a connector-typed field directly?\n  3. For (a) types: extract into frankenterm-core-connector-types alongside the cass-types/caut-types leaf pattern. Both core and the new connector crate depend on the leaf — no cycle.\n  4. For (b) types: rewrite the in-core consumer to hold a generic snapshot or trait-object instead of concrete connector types.\n\nMost likely split (from quick audit): the *TelemetrySnapshot / *Status / *Health structs are leaf-friendly (pure data, Serialize/Deserialize). The *Controller / *Registry / *Mesh impls stay with the cluster.\n\nSuccess criteria:\n  1. policy.rs / config.rs / chaos_scale_harness.rs / runtime_telemetry.rs no longer reference crate::connector_*::* directly — they reach into a leaf-types crate or a generic trait.\n  2. Workspace still compiles via rch.\n  3. Unblocks the connector tier-2 cluster extraction (separate bead, post-leaf-extractions).\n\nDeps: blocked by ft-8cg6y + ft-2z15d landing first (so the leaf-types pattern is proven). Companion to ft-y0loj.5 (parked mcp/connector epic).\n\nReference: docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md §'Survey of the connector_* cluster' + §'Why the cycle is fundamental'.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:05.315428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:45.115770Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","connectors","frankenterm-core","modularity","refactor"]}
 {"id":"ft-dijpe","title":"connector_inbound_bridge: dedup uses attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms as 'now', lets replay bypass the window","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs:495` in `route_signal`:

```rust
if let Some(ref cid) = signal.correlation_id {
    let now_ms = signal.timestamp_ms;
    if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) {
        ...
    }
}
```

The bridge uses `signal.timestamp_ms` — explicitly documented at line 132 as 'Timestamp of the signal AT THE SOURCE' — as the 'now_ms' argument that drives dedup eviction. Every connector (github, slack, webhook, etc.) sends this field and the bridge trusts it as its clock.

## Concrete replay bypass

`SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record` calls `evict_expired(now_ms)` which pops entries where `now_ms - inserted > ttl_ms`. An attacker-controlled upstream can:

1. Send signal X with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=0` → inserted_at=0 in cache.
2. Send signal X again with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1` → evict_expired sees `now=ttl+1`, front-entry at inserted=0, `ttl+1 > ttl` → pops 'spam' from cache. Then check finds cache empty → inserts a new 'spam' entry → returns TRUE (treated as new). Dedup bypassed.

Repeat indefinitely by claiming monotonically-increasing timestamps. Every signal is seen as 'new' and routed to the pattern engine + EventBus, duplicating every downstream side effect (workflow triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Scope

Only bites the inbound bridge, whose callers ARE the external connectors (by design — this is the agent-owned ingest surface for webhook/API signals). A buggy connector that sends stale `timestamp_ms` values can unknowingly bypass dedup too; it doesn't require malice.

Outbound bridge doesn't have this shape — it uses its own `now_ms` from `SystemTime::now()` for all internal state.

## Fix

Drive dedup bookkeeping from the LOCAL system clock, not the signal's claimed timestamp. The signal.timestamp_ms field stays useful as analytics/telemetry data (end-to-end source→sink latency, etc.) but must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

Change `route_signal` to:

```rust
let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .unwrap_or_default()
    .as_millis() as u64;
if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) { ... }
```

## Regression test

- `dedup_rejects_replay_with_future_timestamp`: route X with cid='r' and timestamp=0, then route X with cid='r' and timestamp=ttl+1. Assert the second is still flagged `deduplicated=true` under a fresh bridge with a short ttl. Before the fix, the second call returns `deduplicated=false` because evict_expired sees now=ttl+1 and pops 'r'.

## Severity

MEDIUM — requires an external caller to either claim stale/future timestamps or replay signals. Doesn't crash. But every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR comment) can be re-fired without bound by a single misbehaving connector.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:45:15.242201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.688022Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.687664Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0716e112. Replaced signal.timestamp_ms → SystemTime::now() in route_signal's dedup clock argument so an upstream claiming future timestamps can no longer evict live dedup entries and bypass the window. Regression test bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp (timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 on replay → still flagged deduplicated=true) covers the attacker-replay shape. Verified: 44 connector_inbound_bridge:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-djpi3","title":"[mcp] wa.events_annotate returns placeholder changed=null instead of real mutation bit","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4310-4369, wa.events_annotate always returns McpEventMutationData { changed: None, ... } even though sibling mutation tools wa.events_triage and wa.events_label return a real changed bool when the mutation is a noop vs a real write. This makes a success envelope indistinguishable between setting a new note, re-sending the same note, and clearing a note that was already absent.\n\nImpact: MCP callers cannot tell whether wa.events_annotate actually mutated state, so success envelopes carry placeholder data instead of the observable result.\n\nFix direction: derive a stable changed bit in the tool path (or storage helper) by comparing pre/post note state, and add a regression test that covers both changed=true and changed=false cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:17.651562Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:43:17.733444Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:43:17.733262Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0a865e6f: wa.events_annotate now derives changed bit from diff, not placeholder","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dlyhk","title":"[HIGH] wa.wait_for bypasses ReadOutput policy gate (crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:1618-1684)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:12:43.691734Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:23:10.273730Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:23:10.273458Z","close_reason":"Resolved via 868af5ad: ReadOutput policy gate applied to wa.wait_for before waiting.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","security"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@
+# Round-2 deadlock audit
+
+Re-scan of the surface audited in round-1
+(`docs/review/deadlock-audit.md`, 2026-04-26 02:16) against HEAD.
+Round-1 concluded with zero new beads filed; round-2 confirms
+saturation and adds the 4 sub-crates extracted in the meantime.
+
+## Method
+
+1. `git log --since='10 hours ago'` against the round-1 file set —
+   one commit (`4ccf4175 refactor(runtime): remove runtime_compat
+   alias`), pure rename, no new lock semantics.
+2. Heuristic Python scan for "guard held across non-lock-acquisition
+   await" across `crates/frankenterm-core/src/`,
+   `frankenterm/mux/src/`, `frankenterm/term/src/`.
+3. Same scan extended to the 4 new sub-crates extracted under
+   ft-y0loj.* / ft-j1qjt.* (`replay-types`, `replay`, `ars`,
+   `tantivy`).
+4. Cross-lock acquisition order audit for the
+   `cursors → contexts → tracker` triple in `runtime.rs` to confirm
+   no AB-BA inversion was introduced.
+
+## Findings
+
+### Production code (excluding tests): 1 candidate, low concern
+
+The heuristic returned a single non-test hit in
+`crates/frankenterm-core/src/notifications.rs:325`:
+
+```rust
+let muted = {
+    let storage_guard = storage.read().await;
+    storage_guard
+        .is_event_muted(&identity_key, now_ms)
+        .await
+        .unwrap_or(false)
+};
+```
+
+The read guard is held across `is_event_muted().await`. Verdict: **not
+a deadlock** — `is_event_muted` is a method on the value inside the
+guard (`storage_guard.is_event_muted(...)`), so it does not re-acquire
+the outer lock. The pattern's risk is *writer starvation under
+contention*, not deadlock. The existing comment block (lines 321-323)
+acknowledges the deliberate switch from `lock()` to `read()` to allow
+concurrent mute checks. No bead filed.
+
+### Test code: 12 hits, all expected
+
+All 12 hits in `pool.rs::tests::*` and `snapshot_engine.rs::tests::*`
+are tests that intentionally hold a lock across an `.await` to
+exercise pool capacity / stats / wakeup behavior. Not bugs.
+
+### Cross-lock ordering in runtime.rs
+
+`cursors → contexts → tracker` partial order is preserved across all
+sites that acquire two or three of the triple. No reverse-order
+acquisition exists at HEAD. Confirmed via:
+
+```sh
+rg -n 'cursors\.(read|write)\(\)\.await|contexts\.(read|write)\(\)\.await|tracker\.(read|write)\(\)\.await' crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs
+```
+
+### Sub-crate scan: zero async-lock acquisitions
+
+`rg '\.(lock|read|write)\(\)\.await'` against
+`crates/frankenterm-core-{replay,replay-types,ars,tantivy}/src/`
+returns zero hits. The extracted clusters use only sync mutexes for
